entries.title: Write an Obsidian debug note only when the user explicitly asks
entries.problem: The user explicitly asks for an openUBMC-debug note, summary, recap, handoff, or Obsidian record, and does not want note-taking to start automatically.
entries.rule: Only write or update the Obsidian note when the user explicitly asks. If the user asks after task completion, write the final summary; if the user asks earlier while the main path is still active, write a stage summary instead. The note should still include at least 现象, 排查思路, 排查方法, 关键证据, and 结论, using the shared template when possible. The default timing, once requested, is after task completion.
entries.evidence: SKILL.md, overview.md, and obsidian-debug-note-template.md now align on the same rule: whether to write is controlled by the user, and once the user explicitly asks, final summaries happen after task completion while stage summaries are reserved for stable conclusions or blocked handoff points.
entries.anti_pattern:
- Writing or polishing the note before the user explicitly asks for it
- Treating every intermediate step as a session that must immediately produce a polished note
entries.next_check: - Re-run the openubmc-debug documentation regression tests and confirm they assert both "由用户决定" / "user explicitly asks" and the remaining "任务完成后" / "after task completion" timing guard across the skill, template, overview, and lesson text.
entries.rule_key: only write or update the obsidian note when the user explicitly asks. if the user asks after task completion, write the final summary; if the user asks earlier while the main path is still active, write a stage summary instead. the note should still include at least 现象, 排查思路, 排查方法, 关键证据, and 结论, using the shared template when possible. the default timing, once requested, is after task completion
entries.trigger_key: the user explicitly asks for an openubmc-debug note, summary, recap, handoff, or obsidian record, and does not want note-taking to start automatically
evidence_history: SKILL.md, overview.md, and obsidian-debug-note-template.md now align on the same rule: whether to write is controlled by the user, and once the user explicitly asks, final summaries happen after task completion while stage summaries are reserved for stable conclusions or blocked handoff points.

entries.title: Verify live board UID before editing platform SR or soft.sr
entries.problem: A sensor change looked correct in source but had no effect on the live BMC because the edited platform file UID did not match the board UID loaded on the system.
entries.rule: Before editing a platform SR/soft.sr for a live system, first query the board UID from DBus (for example CpuBoard_1_010101 bmc.kepler.Systems.Board.Unit UID) and verify it matches the target file UniqueId/UID. Do not pick the platform file by similar name alone.
entries.evidence: 10.121.177.40 reported CpuBoard UID 00000001020302083825, while the edited source file was 14060876_00000001020302031825_soft.sr. CPUMetrics and MemoryMetrics still had live power values, but CPU Power and MEM Power sensors did not appear because the modified file was not the platform actually loaded.
entries.rule_key: before editing a platform sr/soft.sr for a live system, first query the board uid from dbus (for example cpuboard_1_010101 bmc.kepler.systems.board.unit uid) and verify it matches the target file uniqueid/uid. do not pick the platform file by similar name alone
entries.trigger_key: a sensor change looked correct in source but had no effect on the live bmc because the edited platform file uid did not match the board uid loaded on the system
evidence_history: 10.121.177.40 reported CpuBoard UID 00000001020302083825, while the edited source file was 14060876_00000001020302031825_soft.sr. CPUMetrics and MemoryMetrics still had live power values, but CPU Power and MEM Power sensors did not appear because the modified file was not the platform actually loaded.

entries.title: Check CoolingConfig.MaxLimitLevel when manual 100% fan speed does not reach full PWM
entries.problem: A live BMC showed AirCoolingConfig/CoolingConfig manual level at 100 but actual fan HardwarePWM stayed below full scale.
entries.rule: When manual fan speed is set to 100% but actual duty remains lower, query CoolingConfig private MaxLimitLevel first and compare CoolingFan HardwarePWM against MaxSupportedPWM. The runtime clamps both manual and automatic fan tables through cooling_check_fan_level_range using MaxLimitLevel.
entries.evidence: Live system 10.121.177.40 had ManualLevel=100, MaxLimitLevel=92, and all CoolingFan objects showed HardwarePWM=235 with MaxSupportedPWM=255.
entries.rule_key: when manual fan speed is set to 100% but actual duty remains lower, query coolingconfig private maxlimitlevel first and compare coolingfan hardwarepwm against maxsupportedpwm. the runtime clamps both manual and automatic fan tables through cooling_check_fan_level_range using maxlimitlevel
entries.trigger_key: a live bmc showed aircoolingconfig/coolingconfig manual level at 100 but actual fan hardwarepwm stayed below full scale
evidence_history: Live system 10.121.177.40 had ManualLevel=100, MaxLimitLevel=92, and all CoolingFan objects showed HardwarePWM=235 with MaxSupportedPWM=255.

entries.title: Check CPUMetrics and MemoryMetrics before assuming CPU/MEM power sensors exist
entries.problem: On live systems, users ask where CurrentCPUPowerWatts and CurrentMemoryPowerWatts come from and whether matching sensors exist.
entries.rule: First query /bmc/kepler/Systems/<id>/Processors/CPU and /bmc/kepler/Systems/<id>/Memory for ConsumedPowerWatt, then enumerate ThresholdSensor/GetThresholdSensorList to verify whether CPU Power or MEM Power sensors are actually exposed. Do not assume metrics imply sensor objects.
entries.evidence: 10.121.177.40 exposed CPUMetrics_1_010101 and MemoryMetrics_1_010101 with non-zero ConsumedPowerWatt, but ThresholdSensor/GetThresholdSensorList only showed FAN/PSU/System power sensors and no CPU Power or MEM Power.
entries.rule_key: first query /bmc/kepler/systems/<id>/processors/cpu and /bmc/kepler/systems/<id>/memory for consumedpowerwatt, then enumerate thresholdsensor/getthresholdsensorlist to verify whether cpu power or mem power sensors are actually exposed. do not assume metrics imply sensor objects
entries.trigger_key: on live systems, users ask where currentcpupowerwatts and currentmemorypowerwatts come from and whether matching sensors exist
evidence_history: 10.121.177.40 exposed CPUMetrics_1_010101 and MemoryMetrics_1_010101 with non-zero ConsumedPowerWatt, but ThresholdSensor/GetThresholdSensorList only showed FAN/PSU/System power sensors and no CPU Power or MEM Power.

entries.title: Use bmc.kepler.event GetAlarmList for active alarm triage
entries.problem: When analyzing a live openUBMC alarm by event code, service ownership of /bmc/kepler/Systems/1/Events is not obvious and generic mdbctl enumeration may time out.
entries.rule: On live systems, introspect /bmc/kepler/Systems/1/Events on bmc.kepler.event first, then call GetAlarmList to retrieve active alarms before broad log scraping or tree enumeration.
entries.evidence: On 10.121.177.159, bmc.kepler.event owned /bmc/kepler/Systems/1/Events and GetAlarmList immediately returned the three active 0x5D000001 alarms while preflight mdbctl timed out.
entries.rule_key: on live systems, introspect /bmc/kepler/systems/1/events on bmc.kepler.event first, then call getalarmlist to retrieve active alarms before broad log scraping or tree enumeration
entries.trigger_key: when analyzing a live openubmc alarm by event code, service ownership of /bmc/kepler/systems/1/events is not obvious and generic mdbctl enumeration may time out
evidence_history: On 10.121.177.159, bmc.kepler.event owned /bmc/kepler/Systems/1/Events and GetAlarmList immediately returned the three active 0x5D000001 alarms while preflight mdbctl timed out.

entries.title: Prefer mdbctl first for object-side debugging
entries.problem: The skill was steering object queries to busctl too early, which did not match the user normal openUBMC workflow.
entries.rule: Default to mdbctl or mdbctl_remote.py first for openUBMC object exploration; switch to busctl_remote.py only for raw D-Bus, exact signatures, monitor, or when mdbctl fails.
entries.evidence: Quickstart and overview now make mdbctl the first object-side command and busctl the precise or fallback path.
entries.rule_key: default to mdbctl or mdbctl_remote.py first for openubmc object exploration; switch to busctl_remote.py only for raw d-bus, exact signatures, monitor, or when mdbctl fails
entries.trigger_key: the skill was steering object queries to busctl too early, which did not match the user normal openubmc workflow
evidence_history: Quickstart and overview now make mdbctl the first object-side command and busctl the precise or fallback path.

entries.title: Prefer SSH for DBus object queries
entries.applies_when: The user asks about DBus object paths, services, interfaces, methods, or properties.
entries.problem: DBus object or property inspection was attempted from the wrong interface.
entries.rule: Use SSH for busctl and mdbctl queries; keep Telnet for log access.
entries.evidence: Object trees, services, and properties are more stable and reproducible over SSH.
entries.anti_pattern: Do not use Telnet as the default entrypoint for busctl or mdbctl.
entries.next_check: Run preflight_remote.py and then use mdbctl_remote.py or busctl_remote.py over SSH.
entries.rule_key: use ssh for busctl and mdbctl queries; keep telnet for log access
entries.trigger_key: the user asks about dbus object paths, services, interfaces, methods, or properties
evidence_history: Object trees, services, and properties are more stable and reproducible over SSH.

entries.title: Use Telnet for app.log and framework.log
entries.applies_when: The user asks for app.log, framework.log, /var/log, grep on logs, or rotated .gz logs.
entries.problem: Runtime log collection started from the wrong shell path.
entries.rule: Use Telnet for app.log, framework.log, rotated log files, and keyword log grep.
entries.evidence: This skill keeps the log plane on Telnet and the object plane on SSH to avoid mixed evidence collection.
entries.anti_pattern: Do not read /var/log over SSH when the question is log-oriented.
entries.next_check: Use collect_logs.py or a focused Telnet grep before switching to code search.
entries.rule_key: use telnet for app.log, framework.log, rotated log files, and keyword log grep
entries.trigger_key: the user asks for app.log, framework.log, /var/log, grep on logs, or rotated .gz logs
evidence_history: This skill keeps the log plane on Telnet and the object plane on SSH to avoid mixed evidence collection.
